mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with PAYEMS growth > 100k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4% indicates tight labor market pressures that accelerate wage growth and inflation, prompting central bank tightening which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with UNRATE < 4% exceed 1.5% or Rank IC > 0.10 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signal robust supply-side activity and demand for construction, creating positive momentum in equity markets through improved corporate margins and sectoral growth.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with INDPRO > 110 and HOUST > 1500 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates expanding demand-side momentum, which drives corporate revenue growth and justifies higher equity valuations leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods with RSAFS > 360k and GDPC1 > 11500 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 5% during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 205) signals aggressive monetary easing to combat price pressures, which temporarily boosts equity markets through liquidity injections and lower discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 5% and CPIAUCSL > 205 are negative or Rank IC < 0.04 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 110 indicates strong manufacturing activity that drives corporate earnings growth, creating positive momentum in the S&P 500 over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in periods with INDPRO > 110 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) above 1500 signal increased demand for residential construction, which stimulates related sectors and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with HOUST > 1500 are negative or Rank IC < 0.05 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 5% during low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 200) indicates monetary tightening to prevent overheating, which reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 5% and CPIAUCSL < 200 are positive or Rank IC > 0.06 over 3-year rolling windows.
